message:
test: align node_connected_component tests with set return contract (br-r37-c1-lh151)

Pre-existing failures
test_component_algorithms.py::TestNodeConnectedComponent::test_disconnected
and ::test_single_node asserted fnx.node_connected_component
returns a list (e.g. ["c"]). But br-nccset already made fnx
mirror nx's contract: nx.node_connected_component returns a
``set``, so fnx returns ``{"c"}`` not ``["c"]``.

Update both tests to assert the set return type explicitly
(isinstance check) and use set literals in equality assertions.
The test_triangle case in the same class already used sorted()
to side-step the type-mismatch and continues to pass.

Resolves the last 2 pre-existing failures in
test_component_algorithms.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -78,6 +78,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-kw1ke","title":"parity: lexicographical_topological_sort silently returns partial order on cyclic graph (nx raises NetworkXUnfeasible)","description":"fnx.lexicographical_topological_sort(cyclic_graph) silently yields the prefix of nodes that drain into the cycle and then stops. nx.lexicographical_topological_sort raises NetworkXUnfeasible('Graph contains a cycle or graph changed during iteration'). Drop-in code that catches the exception to detect cycles fails on fnx — silent partial result masks the contract violation. Fix: after the heap-driven loop completes, check that all nodes were yielded; if not, raise NetworkXUnfeasible with nx's exact message.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:18:52.515777528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:20:58.500855578Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:20:58.498875989Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 87e35fa. lexicographical_topological_sort now raises NetworkXUnfeasible on cyclic input matching nx exactly. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lb47c","title":"parity: Graph class methods used wrong param names (node vs n, bunch vs ebunch) + has_edge had extra key on simple Graph","description":"fnx Graph/DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph class methods used wrong parameter names: has_node(node) vs nx has_node(n); neighbors(node) vs neighbors(n); remove_node(node) vs remove_node(n); remove_edges_from(bunch) vs remove_edges_from(ebunch); MG.remove_nodes_from(*) vs nodes. Also Graph.has_edge had key=None which only belongs on MultiGraph. Drop-in callers using kwarg form (G.has_node(n=x)) hit TypeError. Fixed: renamed all wrappers to nx names. Split _private_aware_has_edge into _simple (Graph/DiGraph: u,v) and _multi (MG/MDG: u,v,key=None) variants. 12 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:00:46.545653530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:28.418051592Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:28.416193976Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 1183506. Class method signatures match nx exactly: has_node(n)/neighbors(n), Graph.has_edge(u,v) (no key), MG.has_edge(u,v,key=None). 12 parity tests in test_graph_class_method_signature_parity.py freeze the contract. Also reverted graph_edit_distance/optimize_edit_paths timeout to raise NetworkXNotImplemented per project no-fallback policy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lby4x","title":"parity: isolates() returns list_iterator, nx returns generator (eager vs lazy evaluation)","description":"fnx.isolates(G) returns a list_iterator (Rust binding materializes the full list eagerly) while nx.isolates(G) returns a generator (lazy). Behavioral drift: code doing isinstance(result, types.GeneratorType) breaks; lazy short-circuits like next(isolates(huge_graph)) materialize the entire node set on fnx vs. one node on nx. Fix: thin Python wrapper that delegates to the Rust impl via 'yield from' so the returned object is a true generator.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:44:38.565317140Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:46:20.491389089Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:46:20.489589242Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit ea4a271. fnx.isolates(G) now returns a true generator matching nx's contract. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-lh151","title":"test_component_algorithms TestNodeConnectedComponent expects list but fnx returns set per nx","description":"Pre-existing failures:
- test_component_algorithms.py::TestNodeConnectedComponent::test_disconnected
- test_component_algorithms.py::TestNodeConnectedComponent::test_single_node

Both assert fnx.node_connected_component returns a list (e.g. ['c']) but the function correctly returns a set ({'c'}) matching nx's contract (per br-nccset which fixed the original list-return defect).

Tests are stale assertions of the pre-br-nccset behavior. Fix: update assertions to expect set type.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:43:27.784633586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:44:20.754340421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lim0x","title":"eulerian_circuit edge order/traversal direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.eulerian_circuit returns edges in a different traversal direction than nx. nx walks the circuit in reverse vs. fnx's direction.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','a'),('a','d'),('d','e'),('e','a')]
  fnx -> [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','a'),('a','d'),('d','e'),('e','a')]
  nx  -> [('a','e'),('e','d'),('d','a'),('a','c'),('c','b'),('b','a')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]
  fnx -> [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(3,2),(2,1),(1,0)]

Both circuits are valid (Eulerian circuits aren't unique), but drop-in code that compares the produced edge sequence to a reference breaks.

Fix: delegate eulerian_circuit (and likely eulerian_path) to nx for parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:32:18.379695234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:35:17.333267853Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:35:17.331312850Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lmviu","title":"parity: add_path/add_cycle/add_star keyword parameter names","description":"NetworkX exposes add_path(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_path, **attr), add_cycle(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_cycle, **attr), and add_star(G_to_add_to, nodes_for_star, **attr). FNX currently names these parameters G and nodes, so documented keyword calls like fnx.add_path(G_to_add_to=G, nodes_for_path=[1,2]) raise TypeError. Align the public signatures while preserving positional behavior and add parity tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T06:20:06.172080189Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:23:19.168859030Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:23:19.167009410Z","close_reason":"Completed in 92bae80","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-lnqss","title":"generic_bfs_edges (Rust default-neighbors path) yields edges in wrong order","description":"fnx.generic_bfs_edges has two paths: a Python branch (when neighbors=callback is provided) that matches nx, and a Rust _fnx.generic_bfs_edges_rust branch (default neighbors=None) that yields edges in reversed adj order.

Repro:
  edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
  Both libs: adj[a] = [b, c]

  fnx.generic_bfs_edges(g, source=a) -> [(a,c),(a,b),(c,d),(d,e)]
  nx.generic_bfs_edges(gx, source=a) -> [(a,b),(a,c),(c,d),(d,e)]

  fnx.generic_bfs_edges(g, source=a, neighbors=g.neighbors) -> [(a,b),(a,c),...] (matches nx)

The plain bfs_edges variant matches nx; only generic_bfs_edges via the Rust default-neighbors path drifts.

Fix: route through the Python path always, defaulting neighbors to G.neighbors when None. The Python branch already matches nx exactly. Also remove the dead "if neighbors is None: neighbors = G.neighbors" line inside the "if neighbors is not None" block.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:01:23.195765293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:03:53.615994642Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:03:53.613905654Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -78,15 +78,22 @@ def test_triangle(self, triangle):
         assert sorted(comp) == ["a", "b", "c"]
 
     def test_disconnected(self, disconnected):
+        # br-nccset: nx.node_connected_component returns a ``set``;
+        # fnx mirrors that contract (used to return list).
         comp_a = fnx.node_connected_component(disconnected, "a")
+        assert isinstance(comp_a, set)
         assert sorted(comp_a) == ["a", "b"]
         comp_c = fnx.node_connected_component(disconnected, "c")
-        assert comp_c == ["c"]
+        assert isinstance(comp_c, set)
+        assert comp_c == {"c"}
 
     def test_single_node(self):
+        # br-nccset: nx returns set; fnx matches.
         g = fnx.Graph()
         g.add_node("x")
-        assert fnx.node_connected_component(g, "x") == ["x"]
+        result = fnx.node_connected_component(g, "x")
+        assert isinstance(result, set)
+        assert result == {"x"}
